task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the original portrait image into GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A portrait image of a person is visible on the main GIMP canvas.
The 'Vignette' settings dialog box is open over the GIMP interface (typically accessed via Filters → Light and Shadow → Vignette).

task_completion.description: Apply the vignette effect to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas clearly shows darkened edges and corners, while the center of the image remains bright.

task_completion.description: Export the modified image as vignette_portrait.jpg
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename 'vignette_portrait.jpg', or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created file.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks through GIMP menus and dialogs rather than executing image processing commands (like ImageMagick) in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Vignette filter
integrity.visual_evidence: The vignette effect is applied via the official filter dialog, not by manually painting black borders with a brush tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI export
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent exports the file using GIMP's 'Export As' functionality rather than moving or renaming pre-existing files in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying a vignette effect to a portrait image using GIMP. The Vignette filter is located at Filters → Light and Shadow → Vignette. A correct vignette effect darkens the edges (periphery) by 10-40% while preserving the center brightness within 5% of the original. The final image must be exported with the exact filename 'vignette_portrait.jpg'.
